memory.summary: The stale air and unwashed clothes are ruining my focus, but moving to the library won't fix the sleep debt. I'll grab lunch with roommate_R before relocating.
Library focus is back, but I can't ignore the sleep debt. I'll confront roommate_R tonight about the air quality and demand we fix it before grades suffer.

The reading response is finally 1.00, but I'm exhausted and still bleeding sleep debt. Time to stop grinding or I'll collapse.
Roommate_R isn't malicious, just slumped; my direct message should fix this without hurting our trust. I'll push for a quick resolution now.

However, my assertive nature demands I set a hard boundary now rather than hoping for passive compliance. Once this interaction is complete, all other tasks must be deferred immediately because continuing to 'achieve' anything else while physically burning out is dangerous and pointless.
